title: Light Croatian Lentil Stew
description: A hearty, earthy, and comforting Croatian-inspired lentil stew with a hint of cocoa, perfect for a sick day. This vegan-friendly dish is made with red lentils and a blend of aromatic spices.
instructions: Saute the onion, garlic, carrots, and celery in a pressure cooker over medium heat until the vegetables are tender.
Add the lentils, cocoa powder, paprika, vegetable broth, tomato paste (if using), salt, and black pepper to the pressure cooker.
Stir well to combine, then close the lid and set the valve to 'sealing'.
Pressure cook on high for 10-12 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or thyme (optional).
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days or freeze for up to 3 months.
